ogTitle: Goat Island vs Gobbagombalin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Goat Island and Gobbagombalin.
heading: Goat Island vs Gobbagombalin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Goat Island, New South Wales and Gobbagombalin, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Goat Island has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Gobbagombalin (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Goat Island or Gobbagombalin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Goat Island has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Gobbagombalin scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Goat Island scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Gobbagombalin scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Goat Island or Gobbagombalin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Goat Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
